title: Microbiology Basics
summary: First-look questions about the tiny lives sharing our world.

questions.prompt: Most microbes living in and on the human body are…
questions.explanation: Most microbes are beneficial or neutral. Only a small fraction can make people sick.

questions.prompt: Which microbe kingdom includes Lactobacillus and other gut helpers?
questions.explanation: Lactobacillus species are bacteria — they're single-celled and often help with digestion.

questions.prompt: Yeast in bread dough turns sugar into…
questions.explanation: Yeast ferments sugars and releases carbon dioxide, which is what makes bread dough rise.

questions.prompt: To see most bacteria, you usually need a…
questions.explanation: Most bacteria are too small for the naked eye but visible at light-microscope tier — around 100× magnification.

questions.prompt: Bifidobacteria in the colon prefer to eat…
questions.explanation: Bifidobacteria feast on dietary fiber — that's why fiber-rich meals tend to support a thriving microbiome.
